insights.headline: Trump Claims Iran War 'Very Close to Over,' Peace Deal Imminent — Markets Rally
insights.summary: Trump declares the US has beaten Iran 'militarily' and the war is 'very close to over,' claiming a peace deal is likely. The announcement sparked market optimism, with Trump asserting the stock market 'is going to boom.' However, the Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed, keeping energy prices elevated.

insights.headline: Strait of Hormuz Effectively Closed — Energy Prices Spike, Markets Whipahed
insights.summary: Top Energy Democrat Martin Heinrich is probing the Trump administration's preparations for a Strait of Hormuz closure. The waterway has been effectively closed since the US launched war with Iran, spiking energy prices and causing extreme market volatility. Iran's sea trade has been completely halted per US assessment.

insights.headline: Wall Street Already Pricing In Peace — Stocks, Dollar, VIX Unwind War Moves
insights.summary: Markets bet the Iran war is effectively over as stocks rally, the dollar strengthens, and volatility indices unwind the war premium. Traders are rotating aggressively into risk assets, with the Bloomberg fear gauge dropping sharply. Bond market also rallying on the 'tone change.'

insights.headline: US Wholesale Prices Surged 4% YoY — Iran War Energy Costs Drive Inflation
insights.summary: US wholesale prices (PPI) surged 4% year-over-year as the Iran war drove up energy costs. PPI rose 0.5% from February to March. The inflation was primarily driven by energy prices spiking due to the conflict, adding pressure on consumers and businesses alike.

insights.headline: Singapore Tightens Monetary Policy as Iran War Fuels Inflation Risks
insights.summary: Singapore's central bank tightened monetary policy in response to inflation risks driven by the Iran war. The war's disruption of energy markets and supply chains has forced central banks in exposed economies to respond preemptively, even as other major banks hold rates steady.

insights.headline: Trump Threatens to Shred UK Trade Deal Over Starmer's Iran War Opposition
insights.summary: Trump has warned that the UK trade deal 'can always be changed' and is threatening to backtrack on it over Starmer's opposition to the Iran war. This adds a new dimension to the geopolitical fallout — a major trade partner resisting US military action, with economic consequences now attached.

insights.headline: Iran Military Threatens Red Sea, Gulf Trade Routes — Global Shipping at Risk
insights.summary: Iran's military is threatening to target Red Sea and Persian Gulf shipping, raising the stakes on global trade disruption beyond the Strait of Hormuz. Chinese suppliers and Mideast importers are already fretting about the war's fallout on trade flows, with shipping insurance costs surging.

insights.headline: Rogoff Warns Dollar Is 20% Overvalued, Markets 'Naive' on Iran War
insights.summary: Harvard economist Kenneth Rogoff has issued a stark warning that the US dollar is approximately 20% overvalued and that financial markets are being 'naive' about the risks emanating from the Iran war. He warns that when the war premium unwinds, currency markets face a significant adjustment.

insights.headline: IMF Cuts Emerging Market Growth, Warns of Heightened Financial Stability Risks
insights.summary: The IMF has cut its growth estimate for emerging economies as the Iran war darkens the outlook. Separately, the IMF also warned that the Middle East conflict is driving up financial stability risks globally. The dual warning highlights the broad macroeconomic impact beyond energy prices.

insights.headline: Bank of America Equities Revenue Jumps 30%, Stock Approaches Two-Month High
insights.summary: Bank of America reported a 30% jump in equities revenue, driving the stock toward a two-month high. The strong results reflect heightened market activity amid war-driven volatility, as trading volumes and deal activity have surged since the Iran conflict began.

positive: Trump: 'The war is very close to over' and 'stock market is going to boom'
Wall Street investors blocking out war volatility — Bloomberg
Bank of America equities revenue up 30%, stock toward two-month high
Bond market rallying on Iran war 'tone change' — traders targeting rally

SpaceX IPO approaching — space investing heating up with record-breaking IPO expected

IMF cuts emerging economies' growth forecast due to war
IMF warns Middle East war driving up financial stability risks globally

Protracted Iran war narrows BOJ's rate hike options — Bank of Japan constrained

North Korean hackers used AI-enabled social engineering in $280M Drift Protocol exploit and new Zerion attack

patterns: Iran war is the dominant narrative across ALL financial markets — equities, bonds, forex, commodities, crypto
Energy/inflation channel is the primary transmission mechanism from war to markets
Strait of Hormuz closure is the key energy supply shock — effectively closed since conflict began
Central banks in exposed economies (Singapore) beginning to tighten in response to war-driven inflation
Trade routes under dual threat: Strait of Hormuz (sea trade halted) + Red Sea/Gulf shipping threatened
Markets unwinding war premium rapidly on peace talk speculation — VIX, dollar, stocks all moving
Dollar strength persists despite Rogoff's 20% overvaluation warning — carries trade still functioning
Crypto finding an independent narrative: IMF global debt warning reframing macro backdrop for bitcoin
Corporate earnings season approaching — energy cost pass-through not yet fully priced in
SEC announces strong FY2025 enforcement results — regulatory focus continues despite crisis
blind_spots: Corporate earnings impact: Energy cost surge from Iran war not yet reflected in Q1/Q2 guidance — earnings season will reveal true damage
Frontier market currency stress: Combined war + tariff pressure on smaller EM economies not captured in mainstream coverage
Supply chain restructuring costs: Medium-term supply chain shifts away from Middle East not priced in to current market forecasts
Agricultural commodity inflation: Tomato prices up sharply (tariffs + war) — broader food price inflation may be underreported
Phone/memory market decline: IDC reports memory crunch + Iran war leading smartphone market contraction — tech demand destruction undercovered
UK diplomatic cost: Trump threatening UK trade deal over Iran war opposition — long-term alliance cost not reflected in market pricing
Pakistan crypto shift: Years-long banking ban on crypto firms lifted — emerging market crypto adoption trend underappreciated
Gold breakout: Testing $4,800 resistance — precious metals may be due for larger move as inflation persists
X/Rakuten integration: X (Twitter) rolling out smart cashtags — Web3 payment integration trajectory underappreciated
Meta data center: $40B commitment with health/rate hike concerns — AI infrastructure buildout economics not fully vetted
